text.title: Tweet Thread Launch Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tweet Thread Launch Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Thread MUST be exactly 7 tweets in the canonical shape (hook → demo → problem → solve → price → CTA → social proof); ad-hoc shapes are rejected.

rationale.source: Jon Yongfook — Indie Launch Threads study

rule.rationale: The 7-shape outperforms ad-hoc launches by 3-5x in indie-hacker X audiences.

rule.statement: Tweet #2 MUST contain a demo gif/video <30s showing core use; static screenshots are rejected.
rule.rationale: Static screenshots get 1/4 the engagement of motion media on launch day.

rule.statement: ≥3 hook variants MUST be tested pre-launch in low-stakes posts; untested hooks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Hook variance is the single largest predictor of launch-thread reach.

rule.statement: Tweet #7 MUST contain a real social-proof quote with logged consent; fabricated or unsourced quotes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Consent + attribution is legally required and is the single largest closer of the launch flow.

rule.statement: Tweet #6 MUST contain exactly one CTA (link to product OR PH launch); multi-CTA tweets are rejected.
rule.rationale: Multi-CTA tweets dilute click-through; single-CTA outperforms by 2x.
